## Intent

{{INTENT_PARAGRAPH}}
<!-- TIP: 2-5 sentences, plain English, present or past tense. State WHAT is changing and WHY. Don't restate the workaround (that goes in §Problem) and don't restate the solution (§Proposed solution). Smell test: a new PM joining tomorrow can read this paragraph cold and tell you what is changing. See PATTERN.md §1. -->

## Problem

{{PROBLEM_PARAGRAPH}}
<!-- TIP: 1 paragraph. Name the form by exact label, name the field, name the default value, name why the default is wrong. See LAP-2039 §"Problem" — "Income Considered field for a co-applicant currently sits inside the Edit form... pre-defaulted to Yes... To actually set it to No, the BCM has to go through a long workaround." Specific is everything. -->

## Current flow that we want to fix

1. {{TEDIOUS_STEP_1}}
2. {{TEDIOUS_STEP_2}}
3. {{TEDIOUS_STEP_3}}
4. {{TEDIOUS_STEP_4}}
5. {{TEDIOUS_STEP_5_OR_MORE}}

{{ONE_LINE_SUMMARY_OF_THE_PAIN}}
<!-- TIP: numbered, painful, every step the operator does today. The point is to make the workaround impossible to defend. See LAP-2039 §"Current flow" — 6 numbered steps + a closing sentence "wastes time and adds extra steps that have no business value". The closing line is the rhetorical kill. -->

## Proposed solution

1. {{ONE_STEP_REPLACEMENT}}
2. **Behaviour on the {{FORM_NAME}} form:**
   - If {{CONDITION_A}}, {{CONSEQUENCE_A}}.
   - If {{CONDITION_B}}, {{CONSEQUENCE_B}}.
3. {{CONSEQUENCE_SUMMARY_E_G_ONE_STEP_INSTEAD_OF_SEVEN}}
4. **Behaviour on the {{OTHER_FORM_OR_EDIT_MODE}} form:**
   - {{FIELD_VISIBILITY_RULE}}
   - **Switching {{A}} → {{B}}:** {{CONSEQUENCE}}
   - **Switching {{B}} → {{A}}:** {{CONSEQUENCE}}
<!-- TIP: SC mandates BIDIRECTIONAL toggle behaviour when the toggle goes both ways — Yes→No AND No→Yes. Both directions get explicit consequences. See LAP-2039 §"Proposed solution" §4 — both switching directions specified, including which fields become editable / disabled / mandatory. Skip §4 only if the change is one-way. -->

## QA Scope

1. {{VERIFY_NEW_FLOW_BOTH_BRANCHES_ON_FORM_A}}
2. {{VERIFY_BOTH_DIRECTIONS_OF_TOGGLE_ON_FORM_B}}
3. {{VERIFY_DOWNSTREAM_TAB_OR_VIEW_REFLECTS_CHANGE}}
4. {{VERIFY_BACK_TO_BACK_TOGGLES_YES_NO_YES_NO}}
5. {{VERIFY_STATE_REFRESH_BEHAVIOUR_NO_HARD_REFRESH_NEEDED}}
6. {{VERIFY_PRIMARY_VS_CO_APPLICANT_SCOPE_DIFFERS_CORRECTLY}}
<!-- TIP: SC QA must include (a) state-refresh — does the downstream tab update without logout/refresh, (b) back-to-back toggles — does state always match the final value, (c) primary-vs-co-applicant scope. See LAP-2039 §"QA Scope" §8 (back-to-back) and §9 (refresh behaviour). These two scenarios catch 80% of regressions. -->

## Open Considerations for BA

{{REGRESSION_RISK_NARRATIVE}}
<!-- TIP: SC's BA section is critical because the unchanged path is where regressions hide. Open with: "The change must not affect the normal flow when {{TOGGLE}} stays {{DEFAULT}}." See LAP-2039 §"Open Considerations" opening — "The change must not affect the normal flow in any way when Income Considered stays Yes." Then list any deviation = regression. -->

Items where BA needs to confirm an assumption or close a gap:

1. **{{PRIMARY_VS_CO_APPLICANT_SCOPE}}.** {{ASSUMPTION_PROPOSAL_AND_IMPACT}}
2. **{{DATA_PRESERVATION_ON_TOGGLE_SWITCH}}.** {{ASSUMPTION_PROPOSAL_AND_IMPACT}}
<!-- TIP: see LAP-2039 §"Open Considerations" §1 (primary applicant scope — lock the toggle for the primary) and §2 (previously filled income data on Yes→No — keep for audit, disabled on screen, do not flow downstream). These two are the canonical SC assumptions; almost every SC has analogues. -->
